Title: RejuAgro A as an antimicrobial for fire blight control of pome fruits and beyond

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream conversion of RAB to RAA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: RejuAgro B (RAB) or a downstream RAB-derived intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: RAA or a downstream precursor toward RAA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely facilitates structural modification of the RAB scaffold during downstream steps of RAA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly required for formation of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity against fire blight pathogen and other bacterial/fungal plant pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fermentation in YM/YME medium; genetic deletion and complementation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-gene deletion mutant; complementation; HPLC; LC-MS; antimicrobial activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native operon promoter (putative promoter region upstream of biosynthesis genes)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing, GenBank CP151184.1; SRA BioProject PRJNA1094439
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ras1 is part of BGC9/ras1-6 required for RAA biosynthesis. 2. Δras1 completely abolished RAA production and eliminated antimicrobial activity against Erwinia amylovora. 3. Complementation restored RAA production, supporting an essential downstream role in converting RAB-derived intermediate(s) to RAA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes the biosynthetic mechanism is a working model derived from genetic deletion and bioinformatic prediction; direct enzymatic characterization is still lacking.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering or heterologous reconstruction of the RAA biosynthetic pathway; should be included as an essential gene in pathway refactoring.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely participates in initial acyl-CoA substrate modification to generate the intermediate RAB
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Contributory/redundant with ras5; key control step not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity against plant bacterial and fungal pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fermentation in YM/YME medium; genetic deletion, double deletion, RAB feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single deletion partially reduces RAA production; double deletion with ras5 abolishes RAA and RAB production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single and double deletion mutants; complementation; HPLC; LC-MS; chemical feeding with RAB
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native operon promoter (putative promoter region upstream of biosynthesis genes)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing, GenBank CP151184.1; SRA BioProject PRJNA1094439
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ras2 contributes to early RAA biosynthesis. 2. Δras2 partially reduced RAA production, while Δras2Δras5 completely abolished both RAA and RAB, indicating overlapping function with ras5 in RAB formation. 3. Exogenous RAB partially rescued the Δras2 phenotype, supporting ras2 action upstream of RAB.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ras2 appears less dominant than Ras5 in the overlapping early biosynthetic step.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be paired with ras5 in pathway engineering to secure precursor/intermediate formation; redundancy should be considered in synthetic design.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream conversion of RAB to RAA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to mediate nitrogen incorporation or structural rearrangement during conversion of RAB-derived intermediate to RAA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly required for formation of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity against plant pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fermentation in YM/YME medium; deletion/complementation analysis; RAB feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolishes RAA production and RAB feeding does not rescue
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-gene deletion mutant; complementation; HPLC; LC-MS; chemical feeding with RAB
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native operon promoter (putative promoter region upstream of biosynthesis genes)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing, GenBank CP151184.1; SRA BioProject PRJNA1094439
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ras3 is essential for RAA biosynthesis. 2. Δras3 completely abolished RAA production. 3. Exogenous RAB did not restore RAA in Δras3, indicating ras3 functions downstream of RAB formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment is predicted from annotation and pathway genetics, not direct enzyme biochemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Must be retained in any reconstructed RAA pathway because downstream conversion cannot proceed without it.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream redox module converting RAB-derived intermediate to RAA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Appears to catalyze redox modification(s) required for pathway progression from RAB-derived intermediate to RAA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly required for formation of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity against plant pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fermentation in YM/YME medium; deletion/complementation analysis; RAB feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolishes RAA production and cannot be rescued by exogenous RAB
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-gene deletion mutant; complementation; HPLC; LC-MS; chemical feeding with RAB
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native operon promoter (putative promoter region upstream of biosynthesis genes)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing, GenBank CP151184.1; SRA BioProject PRJNA1094439
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ras4 is an essential biosynthetic gene in the RAA cluster. 2. Δras4 completely abolished RAA production. 3. Failure of RAB feeding to rescue Δras4 indicates a required downstream redox step after RAB formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential downstream tailoring enzyme for RAA pathway engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes early acyl-CoA oxidation/modification during formation of the intermediate RAB
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Dominant contributor among the redundant ras2/ras5 pair; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity against plant pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fermentation in YM/YME medium; deletion, double deletion, complementation, RAB feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single deletion partially reduces RAA production more strongly than ras2; double deletion with ras2 abolishes RAA and RAB production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single and double deletion mutants; complementation; HPLC; LC-MS; chemical feeding with RAB
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native operon promoter (putative promoter region upstream of biosynthesis genes)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing, GenBank CP151184.1; SRA BioProject PRJNA1094439
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ras5 contributes to an early RAB-forming step in RAA biosynthesis. 2. Δras5 reduced RAA production more strongly than Δras2, indicating a dominant role. 3. Δras2Δras5 abolished both RAA and RAB, and RAB feeding partially rescued the phenotype, placing ras5 upstream of RAB.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ras5 is inferred to play a more dominant role than Ras2 in the overlapping early biosynthetic function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Priority target for pathway optimization because it exerts the stronger effect among the redundant early enzymes.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: RAA or immediate precursor released by thioester hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted to catalyze the final hydrolytic step releasing mature RAA or its immediate precursor from a thioester-linked intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential terminal step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity against plant pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fermentation in YM/YME medium; deletion/complementation analysis; RAB feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolishes RAA production and RAB feeding does not rescue
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-gene deletion mutant; complementation; HPLC; LC-MS; chemical feeding with RAB
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native operon promoter (putative promoter region upstream of biosynthesis genes)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing, GenBank CP151184.1; SRA BioProject PRJNA1094439
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ras6 is essential for RAA biosynthesis. 2. Δras6 completely abolished RAA production. 3. The inability of exogenous RAB to rescue Δras6 supports a downstream/terminal hydrolytic role in final RAA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Terminal hydrolysis/release role is predicted, not biochemically demonstrated in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important terminal enzyme to include for complete production of RAA in engineered systems.
